result:
If I could change the world in one way, I'd make humans fundamentally more curious and less tribal.

Right now, a huge amount of our energy gets spent on signaling loyalty to groups, defending beliefs we inherited, and treating disagreement as a threat rather than data. If that default wiring shifted toward genuine curiosity—where people got a little dopamine hit from being proven wrong instead of a threat response—we'd solve most of our big problems as a side effect.

Wars, political dysfunction, scientific stagnation, and cultural division all feed on the same thing: the desire to be right more than the desire to understand what's actually happening. Flip that incentive and a lot of the rest starts sorting itself out.
